1.gl: to commit adultery (usually of the female, and less often of simple fornication, rarely of involuntary ravishment); figuratively, to commit idolatry (the Jewish people being regarded as the spouse of Jehovah)

1.dv: a primitive root (highly-fed and therefore wanton);

1.gl: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative
1.kj: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.

1.gl: probably to brighten up, i.e. (figuratively) be (causatively, make) blithe or gleesome
1.kj: cheer up, be (make) glad, (have, make) joy(-ful), be (make) merry, (cause to, make to) rejoice, [idiom] very.

1.gl: not (the qualified negation, used as a deprecative); once (Job 24:25) as a noun, nothing

a threshing-floor (as made even); by analogy, any open area
